name: Black Forest Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Stir until well mixed.
2. Add the sugar and cocoa to the dry ingredients; stir until combined.
3. Add the shortening, eggs, milk, and vanilla extract to the dry mixture. Beat the mixture for 3 minutes at a low speed of an electric mixer, scraping the sides of the bowl occasionally.
4. Grease two 9-inch round cake pans with butter or cooking spray. Line the bottoms of the pans with wax paper.
5. Pour the batter evenly into the prepared pans.
6. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 30 to 35 minutes, or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
7. Allow the cakes to cool in the pans for 10 minutes before removing them and placing them on wire racks to cool completely.
8. Split each cake layer horizontally to create four layers. Make fine crumbs from one of the cake layers; set aside.
9. Beat the whipping cream until foamy. Gradually add the sifted powdered sugar, beating until soft peaks form.
10. Place one cake layer on a serving platter and spread with about 1 cup of whipped cream. Add a dollop of cherry pie filling and continue to assemble with the remaining layers.
11. Frost the sides and top of the cake, reserving some whipped cream for garnish.
12. Generously pat the cake crumbs around the sides of the cake (there may be leftover crumbs).
13. Spoon or pipe the remaining whipped cream around the top of the cake and add a spoonful of cherry pie filling in the center.
14. Chill the cake well before serving.
